commit.message:
修正：PermissionError 處理與 retry_interval_cycles=1 無效問題

- conv_admin.py：將 PermissionError 從 ProcessLookupError 分開 catch；
  PermissionError 表示 process 存在但由其他用戶擁有，應將 _daemon_running=True，
  而非 fallback 到 .env，與 BaseDaemon.check_pid_file() 行為一致
- process_daemon.py：retry 條件從 == 1 改為 == 0，使 RETRY_INTERVAL_CYCLES=1
  能正確在每個 cycle 觸發重試；原條件 n%1==1 永遠為 false，導致重試完全失效

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add CliInstallStrategy for local E2E testing and daily smoke tests (#16104)

* Add CliInstallStrategy for unified E2E test CLI installation

Introduce CliInstallStrategy that encapsulates how the Aspire CLI is
detected, configured, and installed inside E2E test Docker containers.

Supports four modes:
- LocalHive: extract localhive archive (ASPIRE_E2E_ARCHIVE env var)
- SourceBuild: native AOT binary + local packages
- PullRequest: PR artifacts via get-aspire-cli-pr.sh
- InstallScript: get-aspire-cli.sh with --quality or --version

Detection priority: env var overrides > CI > SourceBuild > fallback GA

Adds new overloads of CreateDockerTestTerminal and InstallAspireCliAsync
that take CliInstallStrategy instead of DockerInstallMode. Existing
methods preserved for backward compatibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Convert SmokeTests to use CliInstallStrategy

Verified end-to-end locally with LocalHive mode:
- localhive.sh -o /tmp/aspire-linux-arm64 -r linux-arm64 --archive
- ASPIRE_E2E_ARCHIVE=/tmp/aspire-linux-arm64.tar.gz dotnet test
- Full flow: archive extract → channel config → aspire new → aspire run → Ctrl+C
- Test passes in 35s

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Document running CLI E2E tests locally in cli-e2e-testing skill

Add comprehensive 'Running Tests Locally' section covering:
- Quick start for macOS/Linux and Windows/PowerShell
- RID selection table (arm64 vs x64 by host platform)
- Full development workflow loop (change → build → test → debug)
- Install mode env var overrides (LocalHive, quality, version)
- Testing against released/daily versions
- Asciinema recording debugging tips

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Convert TypeScriptStarterTemplateTests to CliInstallStrategy

Remove SourceBuild fallback (FindLocalBundlePath, bundle mounting,
local channel setup). LocalHive provides everything in one archive.
Test is now clean — detect, create terminal, install, test.

Verified locally: passes in 34s with localhive archive.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Log aspire --version after CLI install in E2E tests

Shows the installed version in asciinema recordings for debugging,
making it easy to verify which build is running.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Remove SourceBuild mode from CliInstallStrategy

LocalHive replaces SourceBuild — one archive instead of separate
CLI binary mount, package copy, and bundle symlink. Simpler detection
(no repoRoot parameter needed), fewer code paths.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add daily CLI smoke test workflow

Runs SmokeTests against the latest daily build (ASPIRE_E2E_QUALITY=dev)
on a daily schedule at 6 AM UTC. Also supports manual dispatch with
quality selection (dev/staging/release).

On scheduled failure, automatically creates a GitHub issue with
area-cli and failing-test labels, with dedup to avoid duplicate
issues on the same day.

Uploads asciinema recordings as artifacts for debugging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Default to daily build on CI without PR context

When running on GitHub Actions but without GITHUB_PR_NUMBER (e.g.,
main branch push or scheduled workflow), auto-detect to InstallScript
with quality=dev so tests run against the latest daily build.

commit.message:
test: fix timing-sensitive validation tests for Windows CI

Replace fixed 1000ms sleeps in two client tests with a polling
helper (waitForErrors) that retries every 100ms up to 3000ms.

The root cause: validateFile calls connection.languages.diagnostics.refresh()
which triggers an asynchronous pull-diagnostics round-trip on the client side.
On Windows CI this round-trip can exceed 1000ms, causing the assertions to read
an empty diagnostic collection.

The fix polls until errors appear (fast on Linux) or times out at 3000ms
(safe within the 8000ms test timeout). Test timeouts updated from 5000ms
to 8000ms to accommodate the longer polling window.

Affected tests:
- commandAndDetection: 'Should execute validation command on invalid file'
- realtimeValidation: 'Should still detect errors via manual validation after save'

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[fix](fe) Fix PullUpPredicates losing NULL rows in INTERSECT/EXCEPT (#62299)

### What problem does this PR solve?

Problem Summary:

introduced by #39450

`PullUpPredicates.getFiltersFromUnionConstExprs()` incorrectly removes
`NullLiteral` from UNION ALL constant expressions without compensating
with `OR IS NULL`, producing invalid pull-up predicates.

For example, `SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 3 UNION ALL SELECT NULL`
generates the pull-up predicate `n IN (1, 3)`, which is NOT true for the
NULL row (`NULL IN (1, 3)` evaluates to NULL, not TRUE).

When `InferPredicates` pushes this predicate from one INTERSECT child to
another, the IN filter incorrectly eliminates NULL rows. Since INTERSECT
treats NULL=NULL per SQL standard, this causes incorrect query results
with missing NULLs.

**Root Cause**: In `getFiltersFromUnionConstExprs()`, line 467:
```java
options.removeIf(option -> option instanceof NullLiteral);
```
removes NULL but does not add `OR IS NULL` to compensate.

**Fix**: When NULL constants are present, generate `n IN (...) OR n IS
NULL` instead of just `n IN (...)`, making the predicate valid for all
rows including NULL.

**Reproducing Query**:
```sql
WITH
  tbl0(n) AS (SELECT 1 UNION ALL SELECT 3 UNION ALL SELECT NULL),
  tbl1(n) AS (SELECT 2 UNION ALL SELECT NULL UNION ALL SELECT 1)
(
  SELECT (n * 2) AS n FROM tbl0
  INTERSECT
  SELECT (n * 2) AS n FROM tbl1
);
-- Expected: {2, NULL}  Actual before fix: {2}
```

### Release note

Fixed a bug where INTERSECT and EXCEPT queries could incorrectly drop
NULL rows when the optimizer inferred and pushed down predicates from
UNION ALL constant expressions.

feat(writer): make compact_mode and compression mutually exclusive

compact_mode (single DT block) and compression are incompatible:
DZ blocks are capped at 4MB by the MDF4 protocol, so a single
compressed block cannot be guaranteed for arbitrary data sizes.

commit.message:
EGP Phase Separation Research — Round 3: Per-Patient Settings

EXP-2625: Per-Patient EGP-Aware Settings Optimization (6 patients)

Key finding: 67% of patients have ISF inflated ≥15% by EGP suppression.
Each patient's metabolic system is unique — population model validates
that EGP phase lag exists, but individual parameterization is required.

Per-patient metabolic profiles:
- Patient a: recovery 22.3 mg/dL/hr, ISF inflated 66%, basal possibly low
- Patient f: recovery 8.3 mg/dL/hr, ISF inflated 84%, basal adequate
- Patient g: fast recovery (2.3h nadir), NO ISF inflation (-3%) — unique
- Patient i: dramatic circadian split (day +37 vs night -4.5 mg/dL/hr)

Why recovery doesn't correlate with basal (r=0.085):
The LACK of correlation IS the signal — patients with mismatched
basal-EGP are exactly those who need settings adjustment.

EGP-corrected ISF is 25-66% lower than apparent ISF for most patients.
Corrections from high glucose appear to work better than they do
because EGP suppression adds ~20-30 mg/dL to the observed drop.

Includes 3 new visualizations (fig7-9) and updated research report.

commit.message:
fix: skip mark_done on stale TurnComplete to prevent double-submit

Root cause: after Esc + re-submit, the old stream's TurnComplete arrived
after mark_generating() had already set expecting_turn_start=true. The
TurnComplete handler unconditionally called mark_done(), resetting
is_generating=false. The UI then showed no spinner — the user thought the
1st submit was lost and typed again unnecessarily (need-twice bug).

Fix:
- TurnComplete skips mark_done() when expecting_turn_start=true (a new
  submit is already in flight; this TurnComplete belongs to the old turn).
- TurnComplete also skips the queue-drain when expecting_turn_start=true
  (the new message was already sent via direct submit, not the queue).
- mark_done() now resets expecting_turn_start=false so abort paths leave
  the flag in a consistent state.

commit.message:
fix: normalize URI paths to forward slashes for cross-platform CI

URI.fsPath returns OS-native separators (backslashes on Windows), causing
3 test failures on Windows CI runners in extractWorkspaceFolderPaths and
classifyWatchedFileChanges.

Add uriToPath() utility in textUtils.ts that calls URI.fsPath then
replaces backslashes with forward slashes. Node.js accepts '/' on all
platforms, making internal path comparisons consistent cross-platform.

Fixes:
- serverHandlers/extractWorkspaceFolderPaths: file:///home/user/project
  returned '\home\user\project' on Windows instead of '/home/user/project'
- serverHandlers/classifyWatchedFileChanges: same separator issue for
  fsPath field on ClassifiedFileChange objects
